rulings.Case Title: JOHNNY MURRAY ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Petitioner Johnny Murray To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set Three
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Wednesday, February 26, 2025, Line 4. PETITIONERs JOHNNY MURRAY, BENJAMIN CASTRO, AND RYLEE LIZCANO's Motion By Petitioner Johnny Murray To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set Three. Petitioners' motion to compel the City's further responses to requests for production of documents, set three, is granted. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties.) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: LTC (BVI) LIMITED, A BRITISH VIRGIN ISLANDS VS. BRAUNHAGEY & BORDEN LLP, A LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Wednesday, February 26, 2025, Line 5. DEFENDANT BRAUNHAGEY & BORDEN LLP's MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS. Defendant's motion to quash Plaintiff's subpoena to Precision IT Consulting Inc. and for protective order is referred to the discovery referee. On January 6, 2025, this court ordered appointment of a discovery referee, subject to the parties' meet and confer efforts to identify an appropriate referee. On February 6, the court appointed Hon. Wynne. S. Carvill (ret.) for all discovery purposes. Because there is no dispute that the subpoena to Precision overlaps considerably with pending discovery motions that have been referred to the referee, and because the subpoena implicates many of the attorney-client and third-party confidentiality concerns that led to appointment of the referee, the referee is best positioned to address this motion. The court stays the subpoena to Precision; the court's stay is subject to any future order Judge Carvill may make respecting it. Plaintiff's request for sanctions against Defendant is denied without prejudice to any orders of the referee concerning sanctions. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: LL JOHN DOE OJ VS. DEFENDANT DOE RELIGIOUS ORGANIZATION 1 ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Substituting Party Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 377.32
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Wednesday, February 26, 2025, Line 1. PLAINTIFF LL JOHN DOE OJ's Motion For Order Substituting Party Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 377.32. LL John Doe OJ, plaintiff, died after filing this case. His daughter, Latefia Johnson, seeks to substitute herself as plaintiff in this action as the successor in interest to LL John Doe OJ. The motion is opposed by one of the named defendants, Doe Religious Organization 3 ("Organization 3"). For the reasons stated herein, the court grants the motion. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties.) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Strike Portions Of And Evidentiary Objections To Evidence And Brief Submitted By Plaintiff In Opposition To Motion For Summary Judgment
